world.title: Indonesia extradites Scottish man sought by Spain as crime boss

business.title: Oil rises as investors remain wary US

science.title: NASA Artemis II astronauts to speak from deep space after record-setting flyby
science.description: HOUSTON, April 8 (Reuters) - Four astronauts traveling back from the far side of the moon on NASA's Artemis II mission will speak with reporters in their first press conference from space on Wednesday.

science.title: Mark Carney speaks with Canadian Jeremy Hansen

science.title: Nasa's lunar success sharpens focus on China's 2030 crewed landing goal
science.description: BEIJING — As Nasa's record-breaking Artemis mission bolsters the US path back to the moon, China's bid to land astronauts there by 2030 is taking on greater geopolitical significance and putting pressure on Beijing to meet or beat its timetable.Four US astronauts on the Artemis II mission this week flew past the moon's shadowed far side, travelling deeper into space...

science.title: Artemis II astronauts follow Apollo tradition of naming lunar features after loved ones
science.description: HOUSTON — Lunar love knows no bounds.Now hurtling home from the moon, the Artemis II astronauts took a poignant page from Apollo 8 earlier this week, proposing deeply personal names for a pair of lunar craters.Commander Reid Wiseman and his crew asked permission to name one small, fresh crater after their capsule called Integrity and another after his late wife,...

science.title: Study Identifies Strategy to Overcome Radiation Therapy Resistance in Lung Cancer
science.description: These results show how mitochondrial enzyme dihydroorotate dehydrogenase contributes to radiation therapy resistance .Researchers were able to overcome the resistance with a triple combination therapy including leflunomide, an FDA-approved drug used for rheumatoid arthritis.

ai.title: Waiting for DeepSeek: new model to test China's AI ambitions
ai.description: Despite reports and rumours about its imminent release, DeepSeek's next-generation "V4" model is nowhere in sight. Speculation is also swirling over the geopolitical implications of which computer chips were chosen to train and power the new system: world-leading US designs or made-in-China alternatives that the country is racing to develop.

ai.title: Claude’s New Tool Lets Anyone Create AI Agents Quickly

The platform offers built-in security, monitoring, and long-running workflows, and is now available in public beta., Technology & Science, Times Now

ai.title: OpenAI will reserve portion of IPO shares for retail investors, CFO tells CNBC
ai.description: OpenAI plans to reserve IPO shares for individual investors, CFO Sarah Friar said. The firm, valued up to $1 trillion, may file for IPO in 2026. Large institutional investors ⁠have historically been the primary recipients of IPO allocations, with retail investors typically receiving only 5% to 10% of shares in public ​offerings.

ai.title: US court expedites Anthropic’s legal battle with Department of War
ai.description: The AI start-up sought a stay of action after the Pentagon labelled it a supply chain risk. Read more at straitstimes.com.

ai.title: Appeals court rebuffs Anthropic in latest round of its AI battle with Trump administration
